goal: A developer or operator arriving at the repo cold can understand the architecture, operate the agent, and run a local dev environment using only the README, operator runbook, env-var reference, and ADR index — all accurate to the real code.
why: First phase of the Handoff milestone (REQ-19). The current README describes only the OneDrive integration and predates the agent chat, the 4 pages, the mail stack, auth, encryption, and most env vars; there is no docs/ directory and no ADR index. The client cannot own the system until the docs match reality.
success_criteria: README.md accurately describes Aquavoy as the full agent app (chat + 4 pages), gives a working local-dev path (npm install -> env -> npm run dev), maps the real routes, and links to the runbook, env reference, and ADR index.

docs/operator-runbook.md lets an operator drive the chat, understand confirm/undo (gated destructive set per ADR-003), manage the 12 named mailboxes (IMAP-authoritative per ADR-004), use the OneDrive OAuth connection, and understand the scheduled-email cron.

docs/env-reference.md documents every variable the app reads with required/optional + source, notes secrets are server-only, and clarifies mailbox passwords live in Supabase; .env.example is synced to match.
docs/architecture.md is an ADR index linking ADR-001..004 with one-line summaries and verbatim titles.
No TODO/FIXME/placeholder text remains in any doc produced this phase.

tasks.title: Rewrite README.md as accurate repo orientation
acceptance_criteria: README describes Aquavoy as an internal AI assistant: a single chat surface plus Emails/Files/Prep pages, reading/organizing OneDrive, reading/sending/scheduling email across 12 mailboxes, recalling conversations, searching the web.
README has a Local development section: npm install, fill .env.local (pointing to the env-var reference), npm run dev at http://localhost:3000, and names npm run typecheck and npm run test.
README maps the four real routes: / (Chat), /emails, /files, /prep, plus /login as the auth gate.
README links docs/operator-runbook.md, docs/env-reference.md, and docs/architecture.md by relative path.
Every page/route named exists under src/app/; Supabase setup uses npx supabase.
tasks.action: Full rewrite of README.md. Keep accurate stack facts and Microsoft app-registration steps; add the agent + 4-page reality. Sections: What Aquavoy is, Stack, Pages (route table from src/app/), Local development (install, env, npx supabase migrations, npm run dev), Operating the app (runbook pointers), Documentation (links to the three docs), footer. Point to docs/env-reference.md, do not inline env vars. Any API table must match real route.ts under src/app/api/.

tasks.title: Write the operator runbook
acceptance_criteria: Starting and driving the chat section: log in at /login (principals Wency, Jeanette), open chat at /, type a request, agent runs tools and streams a reply.

Confirm/Undo section: destructive actions (send_email, schedule_email, delete_item, move_item, rename_item) are never auto-executed; agent stages them and shows a confirmation card; Confirm runs it, Cancel drops it, Undo reverses reversible confirmed actions; create_folder and read-only tools are not gated.
Tool-trace row section explains the per-message disclosure of which tools ran (trace surface at /api/traces/[id]).

The 12 company mailboxes section lists all 12 addresses by domain group verbatim from src/lib/mailboxes.ts, states IMAP/SMTP is authoritative per ADR-004 with Outlook for personal mail only, and that mailbox credentials are stored encrypted in Supabase (mail_accounts), not env files; managed from /emails.
OneDrive connection section: delegated OAuth via /api/onedrive/connect and /api/onedrive/callback, tokens auto-refresh, re-connect if a connection stops working.
Scheduled email & cron section: /api/mail/scheduled/run drains every minute guarded by CRON_SECRET; /api/memory/sweep runs every 5 minutes.
No TODO/FIXME or unfinished filler text remains.
tasks.action: Create docs/operator-runbook.md for a non-developer operator in plain language. Pull every mailbox address verbatim from src/lib/mailboxes.ts:31-47. Pull the confirm/undo and gated-tool list verbatim from ADR-003. Pull the IMAP-authoritative/Outlook-personal split from ADR-004. Pull cron schedules from vercel.json. Describe behavior, not invented UI labels.

tasks.title: Write the env-var reference, sync .env.example, and the ADR index
acceptance_criteria: docs/env-reference.md documents every variable the app reads (the 16 in src/lib/env.ts plus CRON_SECRET, GEMINI_MODEL, OPENROUTER_FALLBACK_MODELS) with name, purpose, source, and required-vs-optional; each secret flagged server-only.
The reference states mailbox IMAP/SMTP passwords are NOT env vars but stored encrypted in the Supabase mail_accounts table.

.env.example is updated to include every variable above (adding the 7 missing: CRON_SECRET, GOOGLE_API_KEY, GEMINI_MODEL, OPENROUTER_FALLBACK_MODELS, TAVILY_API_KEY, EMBEDDING_MODEL, EMBEDDING_DIM), matching the existing comment style, no secret values filled.

docs/architecture.md is an ADR index with one entry per ADR (ADR-001 Access-Control Strategy, ADR-002 Durable Memory Architecture, ADR-003 Enforced Confirm/Undo, ADR-004 Keep Both Mail Stacks One Owner Per Operation), verbatim titles, one-line summaries, relative links.
No TODO/FIXME or unfinished filler text remains.
tasks.action: Create docs/env-reference.md as a table (Variable | Required | Server-only | Source/Default | Purpose). Derive the var set from src/lib/env.ts plus the three direct process.env reads (CRON_SECRET, GEMINI_MODEL, OPENROUTER_FALLBACK_MODELS); ignore the incomplete .env.example.
